purpose: Per-frame growing infection that spreads from 2 seed points across a Torus surface; the accumulated infected positions are voxelized and polygonized into a smooth fleshy mesh via the Volume Builder/Mesher stack.
visual_result: Two fleshy growths emerge at the seeds (f60), expand along the torus surface (f120), and fully envelop the surface by f200-333.
input_geometry: Torus (5163) — the surface infection spreads on. Point1 + Point2 (Null, 5140) — initial seed positions.

hosts.role: polygonizes the SDF produced by Volume Builder
consumes voxel data from Object Group, builds SDF
generates per-frame infected point positions, voxelizes via fillgeometry

annotation_storage_pattern: No OM-tag annotations; no scaffold@ section labels in this graph either. Naming carried entirely on container@ and direct-named nodes.

am_sliders.name: Voxel Size
am_sliders.controls: voxel resolution of the fillgeometry output

am_sliders.name: Interior Filter
am_sliders.controls: filter for which voxels count as 'inside' the input mesh

am_exposure_pattern_note: This scene exposes parameters via direct root.in@<hash> ports without floatingio wrappers. This is a SECOND AM-exposure pattern distinct from the floatingio-routed pattern observed in scene 03. Both produce AM-visible parameters; floatingio adds explicit labeling/typing while root-direct surfaces the underlying node port name.

key_connections.purpose: PER-FRAME FEEDBACK CHANNEL 1 — Infection state array self-feedback.
PER-FRAME FEEDBACK CHANNEL 2 — Neighbors state array self-feedback. PROVES Memory supports N independent state channels via numbered ports.
0/1 boolean gate — whether to actually add this candidate (array-growth threshold).
Skip positions already in the infection set so K-NN returns only fresh candidates.
Restrict voxel fill to the Torus surface domain.
Voxel distribution stream consumed directly by the Volume Builder host.

output_routing.via: Geometry Op.Op → root input port; Volume Builder reads root distdataout from fillgeometry
output_routing.host_visibility_path: Object Group emits voxel data → Volume Builder → SDF → Volume Mesher → polygons → display
output_routing.notes: Two parallel outputs from Object Group: (1) Geometry Op for any direct mesh output, (2) fillgeometry.Distribution → Group.distdataout for the Volume Builder consumption.

interesting_types: Multi-channel Memory primitive — N independent state arrays via numbered ports (_0, _1, ...)
fillgeometry.Distribution stream — direct graph-to-Volume-Builder bridge without intermediate caches
Exclude Index port on nearestneighbor — array of indices to skip
Per-frame point cloud as a state array (vs the per-vertex chemical scalar of scene 03)

core_idea_nodes.name: Memory
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Multi-channel state buffer carrying both Infection (the spreading state) and Neighbors (per-frame neighbor cache). PROVES the Memory primitive scales to N independent state channels.

core_idea_nodes.name: Append Elements
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Implements array-growth-per-frame: state[t] = state[t-1] ⊕ filter(neighbors). Combined with compare gating, this is the canonical 'spreading state' primitive.

core_idea_nodes.name: Closest Points
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Same K-NN pattern as scene 03 but with Exclude Index input — skips already-infected positions to keep candidates fresh per iteration.

core_idea_nodes.name: Volume Voxel Fill
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: The graph→Volume Builder bridge primitive. Converts a point cloud into voxel distribution data Volume Builder can consume directly. Unlocks the entire Volume Pipeline as a graph output target.

core_idea_nodes.name: Start Points
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Initial seed array (Point1.position + Point2.position) — feeds Memory's initial state. Without this, Memory has no infection to spread from.

scaffolding_nodes: container × 4 (Strength, Infection, Neighbors, Positions — passive value-holders)
multransform_5 / combine / mat / sqrpart / sqrtrans / vectrans (root template internals)
what_is_clever: Multi-channel Memory: TWO simultaneously self-feedback'd state arrays (Infection + Neighbors). Generalizes to any multi-species/multi-state simulation.
Exclude Index on nearestneighbor: keeps K-NN candidates fresh per iteration, preventing infinite re-processing of already-known points.
fillgeometry as the graph→volume-pipeline bridge: no intermediate file/cache; direct point-cloud-to-SDF wire. Volume Builder reads the graph's output stream live.
Two AM-exposure patterns coexist: floatingio (labeled+typed) and root-direct (raw passthrough). Different ergonomics for different use-cases.
Sequential-only Memory evaluation is the simulation's signature: real time-dependent state, not just a procedural evaluation.

gotchas.issue: Memory primitive only updates on SEQUENTIAL frame stepping. Direct SetTime(N) jumps produce 0 evolution.
gotchas.evidence: Volume Mesher cache: jump-to-f120=0 points; sequential 0→...→30 produces 3858 points. Logged for gotcha #57.
gotchas.workaround: In MCP test loops touching simulation scenes, ALWAYS step contiguously. Never jump in time.

gotchas.issue: All OM objects load with vis_e=2 (UNDEF/hidden). Without unhiding, scene appears empty.
gotchas.evidence: walk(doc.GetFirstObject()) showed every object had vis_e=vis_r=2 on fresh load.
gotchas.workaround: Set ID_BASEOBJECT_VISIBILITY_EDITOR=0 on every object after load. Universal across DRuckli scenes.

gotchas.issue: BaseDraw has no SetMg method in C4D 2026; can't programmatically frame the perspective camera.
gotchas.workaround: Create a CameraObject (Ocamera), SetAbsPos + SetRelRot on it, bd.SetSceneCamera(cam). Already documented in CLAUDE.md memory.

gotchas.issue: Scene has zero floatingio nodes — AM-exposure happens via root-direct ports.
gotchas.evidence: All floatingio nodes count = 0; Voxel Size + Interior Filter wire from Root.in@<hash> directly to fillgeometry inputs.
gotchas.workaround: When mining AM sliders from a Neutron scene, walk both floatingio nodes AND root.GetInputs() ports. Don't miss the root-direct pattern.

rebuild_recipe: 1. Create Volume Mesher → Volume Builder → Scene Nodes Generator (180420500) host stack in OM (parent → child → grandchild).
2. Add seed Null objects (Point1, Point2) and an input Torus to the OM as siblings of the Volume Mesher.
3. Inside the Neutron graph: add legacyobjectaccess × 3 to bridge Point1, Point2, Torus into the graph.
4. Add buildfromvalue ('Start Points') with initial value = [Point1.position, Point2.position].
5. Add memory primitive carrying 2 state channels: Infection (state[]) and Neighbors (cache[]); wire .out._0 → .in._0 and .out._1 → .in._1 for each.
7. containeriteration over Memory.Infection — for each currently-infected position:
a. nearestneighbor on Torus geometry with Query Position=current iteration, Exclude Index=already-known-indices
b. compare (Greater Than) gates whether to accept this candidate (e.g., distance < threshold)
c. append (Append Elements) adds candidate to a working array, gated by compare.out
8. After containeriteration: feed the grown array back to Memory.Infection (closing the per-frame loop).
9. fillgeometry: Geometry=Torus, Voxel Size + Interior Filter = root AM ports. Distribution → root.distdataout for Volume Builder consumption.
10. Volume Builder reads .distdataout, Volume Mesher polygonizes — producing the visible spreading-mesh output.
11. Expose Voxel Size + Interior Filter as root-direct AM ports (no floatingio wrapper).

minimal_reproducible_subgraph.name: array_growth_per_frame
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.purpose: Per-frame state array that grows by appending neighbor-query candidates gated by a threshold. The canonical 'spreading' simulation primitive.
nodes: legacyobjectaccess (initial seed null OR buildfromvalue with hardcoded seed positions)
buildfromvalue (Start Points — initialize state array from seeds)
memory (state buffer; out._0 → in._0 self-feedback for the Infection array)
nearestneighbor (with Exclude Index to skip already-known positions)
compare + append (gate + grow the state array)
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.value_proposition: Spreading-state is the canonical primitive for fire propagation, infection, lichen/moss growth, crystal nucleation, flood-fill, BFS-style traversal on irregular geometry, ivy/vine growth. Combined with the volume_pipeline pattern (fillgeometry + Volume Builder + Volume Mesher) it becomes production-ready volumetric growth.
secondary_recipes: R10_volume_pipeline_bridge — fillgeometry + Volume Builder + Volume Mesher 3-stage host stack
R11_multi_channel_memory — Memory with N state channels via numbered ports
blockers_to_recipe_implementation: Memory multi-channel port wiring needs synthesizer support (numbered ports _0, _1, ...)
fillgeometry node not yet exposed via cinema4d-mcp scene_nodes_add_node
Exclude Index port mapping on nearestneighbor not yet documented
Sequential-step evaluation in MCP test loops needed for any simulation scene

lessons_for_cinema4d_mcp: fillgeometry@ is THE graph→Volume-Builder bridge. Document and recipe-fy.
Memory's port count is variable — supports N state channels via numbered ports. Synthesizer must handle this.
Sequential-only simulation evaluation is a runtime constraint affecting viewport_screenshot(frame=N), render_frame, scene_assert. They MUST step sequentially, not jump.
Detect AM exposure WITHOUT floatingio wrapper. Walk root.GetInputs() directly; not all hosts use the floatingio idiom. Both patterns coexist in the same graph engine.
Visibility=2 on fresh load is universal across DRuckli scenes — needs an auto-fix in load helpers.
BaseDraw.SetMg doesn't exist in C4D 2026 — must create a CameraObject for programmatic camera control.
